qa_pairs.question: What is JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: JupyterLab is the recommended general-purpose user interface for use on a JupyterHub.

qa_pairs.question: What functionalities does a JupyterLab server offer?
qa_pairs.answer: From a JupyterLab server, you can manage your remote files and folders, and launch Jupyter applications like a terminal, Python 3 notebooks, RStudio, and a Linux desktop.

qa_pairs.question: Can I add custom kernels to JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can add your own 'kernels', which appear as application tiles. For configuration details, refer to the 'Adding kernels' section in JupyterNotebook documentation.

qa_pairs.question: What are the primary ways to launch JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Traditionally, JupyterLab is launched using JupyterHub. More recently, some sites have deployed Open OnDemand, which can also launch the interface.

qa_pairs.question: What does the "Fully-featured" column signify in the JupyterLab launching table?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Fully-featured" column indicates whether the available JupyterLab interface includes all the described features.

qa_pairs.question: Is JupyterHub available on the Fir cluster, and is it fully-featured?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, JupyterHub is available on Fir and is fully-featured. You can access it via https://jupyterhub.fir.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Can I launch JupyterLab on the Killarney cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, neither JupyterHub nor Open OnDemand are available for launching JupyterLab on Killarney.

qa_pairs.question: Is JupyterHub available on Narval, and is it fully-featured?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, JupyterHub is available on Narval and is fully-featured. You can access it via https://jupyterhub.narval.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: How can I launch JupyterLab on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Nibi cluster, you can launch JupyterLab via Open OnDemand, which is available at https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Is JupyterHub available on Rorqual, and is it fully-featured?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, JupyterHub is available on Rorqual and is fully-featured. You can access it via https://jupyterhub.rorqual.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Can I launch JupyterLab on the tamIA cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, neither JupyterHub nor Open OnDemand are available for launching JupyterLab on tamIA.

qa_pairs.question: How do I launch JupyterLab on the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Trillium cluster, you can launch JupyterLab through Open OnDemand at http://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca/, by requesting "Jupyter Lab + Alliance software extensions".

qa_pairs.question: Can I launch JupyterLab on the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can launch JupyterLab on the Vulcan cluster via Open OnDemand, available at https://vulcan.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to install JupyterLab in a virtual environment yourself?
qa_pairs.answer: No, installing JupyterLab yourself in a virtual environment is not recommended, as you will not benefit from any of the pre-configured applications.

qa_pairs.question: What does the JupyterLab dashboard show when opened in recent clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When you open JupyterLab in one of our most recent clusters, you will be presented with a dashboard pre-populated with launchers for applications like Python 3.11, LibreQDA, Mate Desktop (VNC), OpenRefine, RStudio, VS Code, and XFCE4 Desktop (VNC). You may also find links to Globus, job portals, and documentation.

qa_pairs.question: How do loaded modules affect the JupyterLab dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: By loading modules, new launchers for corresponding applications will appear in the dashboard.

qa_pairs.question: How do I close a JupyterLab session?
qa_pairs.answer: To close your session, navigate to the 'File' menu in the top menu bar.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Hub Control Panel' option in the 'File' menu?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Hub Control Panel' allows you to manually stop the JupyterLab server and its corresponding job on the cluster, which is useful for starting a new server with different resource allocations.

qa_pairs.question: What happens when I select 'Log Out' from the 'File' menu?
qa_pairs.answer: Selecting 'Log Out' will end your session, which also stops the JupyterLab server and its corresponding job on the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the tool selector located in the JupyterLab interface?
qa_pairs.answer: The tool selector is located on the left side of the JupyterLab interface.

qa_pairs.question: What is the function of the tool selector?
qa_pairs.answer: The tool selector changes the content displayed in the frame on the right side of the interface.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'File Browser' used for in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'File Browser' (represented by a folder icon) is used to browse your home, project, and scratch spaces, and also to upload files.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Running Terminals and Kernels' tool?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Running Terminals and Kernels' tool (represented by a stop icon) is used to stop active kernel sessions and terminal sessions.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'GPU Dashboards' tool provide?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'GPU Dashboards' tool (represented by a GPU card icon) provides access to resource monitoring options if your job utilizes GPUs.
